config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights are treated as secondary, abuse becomes too easy to justify. In my view, society is strongest when it protects personal dignity, freedom, and due process, even when that is inconvenient. Public interest matters, but it should not be used as a blanket excuse to override basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena bagi saya itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya cenderung melihat hak individu, kebebasan berbicara, dan due process sebagai pagar utama yang justru membuat masyarakat sehat dan sah secara moral. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa dikesampingkan setiap kali mayoritas merasa terganggu, maka minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer akan paling mudah dirugikan. Jadi, menurut saya, masyarakat seharusnya dilayani melalui perlindungan hak individu, bukan dengan menempatkannya di bawahnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual, because in practice that often becomes a convenient excuse for abuse and authoritarian behavior. From an Indonesian perspective, I know how easily “public order” or “the common good” can be used to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or ignore due process, so I do not trust that logic by default. A healthy society is not built by sacrificing personal rights whenever it is convenient; it is built by protecting dignity, freedom, and fairness for everyone, especially people who are unpopular or vulnerable. Society matters, but it should be the result of respecting individuals, not the justification for overriding them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, dan justru hak-hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara dan due process yang melindungi masyarakat agar tetap adil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu bisa dikesampingkan kapan saja, minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer akan paling rentan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu, bukan dengan menempatkannya di bawah kehendak mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the claim that society should take priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “public interest” can become a cover for silencing people and protecting power. Society is not truly strong if it can only function by sacrificing dissenters, minorities, or anyone who is inconvenient. Of course there are moments when individual freedom has to be balanced with public safety, but that is not the same as giving society automatic priority. For me, rights are the foundation of a fair society, not a luxury that can be removed whenever the majority feels like it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang Amerika, itu terlalu sering jadi alasan untuk membiarkan negara atau mayoritas melampaui batas. Saya juga tidak setuju dengan anggapan bahwa “kepentingan umum” otomatis lebih moral; tanpa hak individu yang kuat, kebebasan berbicara, due process, dan perlindungan minoritas mudah dikorbankan atas nama keteraturan. Tentu ada keadaan darurat yang menuntut pembatasan terbatas, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan cek kosong bagi kekuasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu demi stabilitas, melainkan yang membangun stabilitas dengan menghormati hak setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
